item.title: AWS token parsing silently sets secretKey to undefined on malformed input
item.intent: A malformed AWS token produces an immediate, actionable error at parse time — never a cryptic failure at API call time.
item.description: src/cloud/provisioning/providers/aws.ts:113 splits the token on ':' without validating the result. If the token contains no colon or only one part, secretKey is set to undefined and causes cryptic failures during API signing with no actionable error. Steps: (1) replace bare destructuring with a parts array; (2) validate parts.length === 2 and both parts are non-empty strings; (3) throw Error('AWS token must be in format ACCESS_KEY_ID:SECRET_ACCESS_KEY') on failure; (4) add test cases for: no colon, one part, empty access key, empty secret key. Scope: ~5 lines in aws.ts, no logic change to valid paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Malformed token (no colon) throws immediately with clear message | Empty access key or secret key throws with clear message | Valid token parses correctly — no regression | Test cases cover all invalid formats
Malformed token with no colon throws Error referencing expected format
Empty access key or empty secret key throws immediately with clear message
Valid colon-separated token parses correctly — no regression on valid AWS paths
Token with only whitespace parts is rejected (trim before check)
